layout.wsTitleConnected: WebSocket connected — status and sessions update in real time.
layout.wsTitleDisconnected: WebSocket disconnected — retrying automatically; click to reload the page.
layout.wsTitleAgentReady: OpenPawlet chat WebSocket is connected (streaming channel).
layout.wsTitleLivePushOff: Console /ws push is off. Open Chat to link the agent WebSocket, or set VITE_CONSOLE_WS_URL for API push.

app.crashHint: Try reloading the page. If the problem persists, check the browser console.

agentsHub.pageSubtitle: Manage individual agents, collaborative teams and runtime processes in one place.
agentsHub.agentTeamsSubtitle: Manage individual agents and collaborative teams.

knowledgeHub.pageSubtitle: Skills, external MCP tools, plus the bot's long-term memory and persona.
knowledgeHub.memoryAndProfileSubtitle: Manage long-term memory, knowledge base, and the agent persona profile in one place.

observabilityHub.pageSubtitle: Traces, activity events, runtime logs and queue snapshots.

chat.inputHint: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for new line
chat.contextTooltip: Context: estimated / window · % used (refreshed via /status after each reply)
chat.contextAssembledEmptyHint: Send a message to record the assembled LLM context.
chat.title: Chat
chat.subtitle: Work with OpenPawlet
chat.heroTitle: Hello, how can I help you today?
chat.heroSubtitle: Ask anything about your projects, code, or environment. I'll use your OpenPawlet setup to help.
chat.suggestionReviewText: Review the code structure of the current repository.
chat.suggestionAutomationText: What automation options can be integrated?
chat.suggestionScriptText: Write a simple Python script for me
chat.renameSessionHint: Optional: set a clearer title. Leave blank to use the auto-generated summary title.
chat.sessionsLoadError: Could not load sessions. Check your connection and try again.
chat.sessionsEmpty: No sessions yet. Send a message below to start.
chat.toastWsSendFailed: Failed to send over OpenPawlet WebSocket. Ensure OpenPawlet is running and the ws channel is enabled.
chat.toastWsNotReady: OpenPawlet WebSocket is not connected. Ensure OpenPawlet is running and the ws channel is enabled.
chat.showSubagentSessionsHint: Sub-agent transcripts are hidden by default. Toggle on to surface them under the main agent list and inspect their full execution.
chat.sessionDisplayModeHint: Switch sidebar density. Off shows title only; on also shows time, message count, and action buttons.
chat.openpawletWsDuplicateTab: This chat_id is connected in another tab or window; this page was closed and will not auto-reconnect. Refresh or close duplicate pages.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Monitor your OpenPawlet assistant
dashboard.restartContent: Are you sure you want to restart the bot?
dashboard.usageDailyByCalendar: Daily totals are grouped by calendar day in {{tz}}.
dashboard.noUsageData: No usage data yet. It will appear after you chat with a model.
dashboard.botRequired: Cannot determine the current bot. Select a bot in the sidebar first.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure your OpenPawlet preferences
settings.envSaved: Environment variables saved. The OpenPawlet process restarts briefly so changes apply on the next boot.
settings.channelsCardHint: Global options under channels in config.json. They apply to all channels unless a specific channel overrides them in its own block.
settings.channelsSendProgressExtra: Stream incremental assistant text to the channel.
settings.channelsSendToolEventsExtra: Structured tool_calls / tool_results (e.g. WebSocket tool_event frames).
settings.channelsSendReasoningContentExtra: Push assistant reasoning_content on outbound sends; session history is unchanged.
settings.channelsTranscriptionLanguageExtra: ISO 639-1/639-2 lowercase code (2–3 letters), e.g. en, zh. Leave empty to let the backend choose.
settings.channelsSessionTurnLifecycleExtra: Channel names that receive _session_turn_event start/end on the outbound bus (tags).
settings.agentDefaultsHint: Defaults match agents.defaults in config.json; profiles or session settings may override. Expand Default configuration and Advanced to edit everyday fields and schema-aligned extras separately.
settings.contextBlockLimitExtra: Optional; leave empty for no limit (null).
settings.disabledSkillsPlaceholder: Search and select skills to disable
settings.modelExtra: Pick a suggested model or type provider/model (e.g. anthropic/claude-opus-4-5)
settings.providerExtra: Pick an LLM provider or leave as 'auto' for automatic detection
settings.timezoneExtra: Pick a common IANA zone; if your config already uses another zone, it appears at the top. Saving writes config.json and restarts OpenPawlet briefly. Other zones: edit config.json.
settings.timezonePh: Common time zones (optional; clear for UTC)
settings.maxHistoryMessagesHint: Unconsolidated messages sent to the model per turn. 0 keeps full history; set a positive value to cap context size.
settings.providersWarnDesc: API keys are stored in your config file. Saving restarts OpenPawlet briefly; in-flight requests may fail during the restart.
settings.restrictWorkspaceDesc: When enabled, file and shell operations are limited to the workspace directory
settings.toolsExecAllowedEnvKeysExtra: Environment variable names passed through to subprocesses (in addition to mirrored .env keys marked Exec).
settings.toolsMyEnableExtra: Exposes lightweight runtime inspection to the agent.
settings.toolsSsrfHint: CIDR ranges exempt from SSRF checks for web_fetch (e.g. 100.64.0.0/10 for Tailscale).
settings.channelsNoneDesc: Add channel configurations to your config file.
settings.envAlertDesc: These variables are written to .env and loaded when the bot starts.
settings.envAlertDetail: Saves write .env and restart the OpenPawlet process so values load on the next boot; values are stored as plain text. Enabling "Exec" mirrors a variable into tools.exec.allowedEnvKeys so it is visible to commands the agent runs via the exec tool.
settings.envExecVisibleHint: Allow this variable to reach commands the agent runs via the exec tool (mirrored into tools.exec.allowedEnvKeys). Off by default to keep the sandbox tight.
settings.errExtraHeadersJson: Extra Headers for "{{name}}" is not valid JSON. Fix it before saving.
settings.errExtraHeadersObject: Extra Headers for "{{name}}" must be a JSON object (string keys and string values).
settings.errExtraHeadersString: Extra Headers for "{{name}}": value for "{{key}}" must be a string.

llmProviders.title: LLM provider instances
llmProviders.introDesc: Each instance has its own API keys and base URL. Bind agents to instances; multiple keys rotate on errors and configurable triggers fail over to backup instances.
llmProviders.empty: No provider instances yet. Click Add to create your first one — typical setup: one instance per key, then chain them via the failover field.
llmProviders.testTip: Send a tiny ping request using this instance's credentials.
llmProviders.confirmDelete: Delete instance "{{name}}"? Agents bound to it will fall back to the main provider.
llmProviders.fieldIdExtra: Stable, unique. Auto-generated when blank. Cannot change after save.
llmProviders.fieldDescriptionPh: Short note shown when agents pick this instance
llmProviders.fieldApiKeysExtra: Add one row per credential. First key is the default; the runtime rotates to the next when triggers fire (e.g. timeout). Existing keys are masked — use the eye icon to reveal one briefly.
llmProviders.keysEmpty: No API keys yet. Add one below to enable this provider.
llmProviders.keyConfirmDelete: Remove this API key? Agents bound to this instance will fall back to the next configured key.
llmProviders.keyValueRotatePh: Paste a new key to rotate (leave blank to keep)
llmProviders.defaultBadgeHint: Used by every Agent that has no explicit provider binding.
llmProviders.enabledExtra: Disabled instances are skipped by routing and can't be picked as default.
llmProviders.isDefaultExtra: Pick the workspace-wide fallback when an Agent has no explicit binding. Only one instance can be the default.
llmProviders.fieldFailoverChainExtra: When this instance keeps failing for the configured triggers, the runtime tries these instances in order.
llmProviders.fieldFailoverChainPh: Pick one or more backup instances
llmProviders.fieldFailoverTriggersExtra: Conditions that trigger key rotation and instance switch.

agentProfile.providerInstanceExtra: Bind this agent to one configured provider (multi-key + fail-over). Falls back to main when unset.

websocketDebug.hint: Raw frames when Chat connects to OpenPawlet via /OpenPawlet-ws (includes ready, deltas, etc.).
websocketDebug.empty: No frames yet (open Chat and start a session)

activity.title: Activity
activity.subtitle: View bot activity feed and events
activity.detailHint: Raw metadata from the agent observability buffer.

memoryAndProfile.pageSubtitle: Long-term memory, history, and SOUL, USER, and other bootstrap files.

mcpAndSkills.pageSubtitle: Workspace skill bundles and MCP tool servers to extend the agent.

observability.title: Agent observability
observability.subtitle: Recent agent runs, model calls, and tool executions from the OpenPawlet in-memory buffer (per bot gateway). Click Refresh to load the latest data.
observability.traceTableHint: Newest first. Use trace_id to narrow one conversation run.
observability.pageTagline: Inspect runs, LLM, and tool spans in a trace-style table — inspired by LangSmith runs.
observability.emptyFiltered: No events match the current filter.
observability.rowClickHint: Click a row to open details. Events with the same trace_id are grouped.
observability.groupBySessionHint: Grouped by session, then by trace. Each trace has a timeline (Gantt) and detail rows. Newest first.
observability.ganttAria: Gantt-style timeline of spans in this trace
observability.ganttTooltipHint: Click the bar to open full details
observability.groupTraceInHeader: Trace id is on the group row above
observability.dashboardHint: Metrics and distribution use the last loaded batch; the stream below is newest on top and refreshes about every 8s.
observability.timelineSourceError: Failed to read trace data from local JSONL
observability.timelineEmpty: No events yet. Chat with the bot, then refresh.
observability.activityFocusHint: Live event stream below (newest first). Use the header for batch counts and filters.
observability.chartNoData: Not enough data for a distribution yet.

logs.title: Runtime logs
logs.subtitle: Local runtime log file for the OpenPawlet console API (instance data directory, logs/ next to config.json).
logs.emptyFile: This log file is not on disk yet. It appears after you start the console server.

workspace.pageSubtitle: Browse and edit files in the bot workspace
workspace.selectFileHint: Select a file from the list

botProfile.title: Profile
botProfile.subtitle: Bootstrap files: SOUL, USER, HEARTBEAT, TOOLS, AGENTS

mcp.subtitle: Manage Model Context Protocol servers to extend AI capabilities
mcp.emptyLead: MCP connects external tools and data. After you add {{mcpServers}} under {{tools}} in your config, status appears here.
mcp.whatIsBody: An open protocol for AI to call external tools safely—common uses include files, browsers, repos, databases, and more.
mcp.step3: After saving, OpenPawlet restarts briefly so the new MCP configuration is active.
mcp.nameInvalid: Use letters, digits, underscores, or hyphens; must start with a letter or digit
mcp.argsJsonHint: Must be a JSON array of strings
mcp.argsInvalid: Args must be a JSON array of strings
mcp.stepFormFirst: Click Configure MCP to open the dialog, add servers, choose stdio or URL, then click Save MCP configuration.
mcp.stepSettingsExtra: Other tool options (e.g. workspace restriction) are under

cron.pageSubtitle: Manage cron jobs that target specific agents, skills or tools on schedule
cron.emptyDesc: No scheduled tasks yet. Add one to run the agent on a schedule.
cron.fieldAgentPh: Pick the agent that handles this task
cron.fieldAgentTip: Leave empty to use the bot's main agent; pick a sub-agent to delegate.
cron.fieldSkillsTip: When the job fires, the agent is hinted to use these skills first.
cron.fieldMcpServersTip: Restrict the MCP servers callable for this task.
cron.fieldToolsPh: Type a tool name and press enter (e.g. web_search)
cron.fieldToolsTip: Optional: explicitly call out tools to use.
cron.fieldPromptPh: Instructions sent to the agent when the job fires, e.g. 'At 9:00 every day, summarize yesterday's sales into markdown'.
cron.fieldPromptTip: Multi-line supported; target metadata is forwarded to the prompt context.
cron.fieldWindowEnabledTip: When on, only schedule the job within the chosen window.
cron.fieldStartAtAtTip: 'Run once' uses its own time as the start.
cron.windowNote: The active window is stored in the task message metadata. Outside the window the job is marked as expired or pending on the card.
cron.scheduleSummaryTip: Human-readable summary of the chosen schedule
cron.fieldMessagePh: Instructions the agent receives when the task fires
cron.summaryHourlyAt: Runs at minute {{minute}} of every hour
cron.summaryMonthlyAt: Runs on day {{day}} of every month at {{time}}

channels.pageSubtitle: Manage integrations and connectivity for your bot
channels.emptyDesc: Open Edit on a card to add credentials and enable a channel
channels.hintSelect: Click a card for details · Edit to configure
channels.configAlert: Changes are saved to config.json. OpenPawlet restarts briefly so they take effect (the page usually reconnects automatically).
channels.disableConfirmDesc: OpenPawlet will restart briefly after saving so the channel change takes effect.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Manage built-in and workspace skills
skills.registryDescription: Registry URL (JSON format). Load skills and install with one click.
skills.registryEmpty: No skills found or registry empty
skills.restartTitle: Changes apply on the next turn
skills.restartDescription: Enabling/disabling skills or editing skill content takes effect when the next conversation turn rebuilds its context. In-flight turns keep their current skills.
skills.restartCompact: Saved changes apply on the next conversation turn; in-flight turns are unaffected.
skills.nameRule: Only letters, numbers, underscore, and hyphen
skills.bundleSkillRootHint: Matches the Name field above (folder under .cursor/skills/)
skills.bundleRootSelectedHint: Skill bundle root (folder). Edit the name in the Name field above; expand the tree to edit SKILL.md and other files.
skills.bundleHint: SKILL.md is the entry doc; use “Add directory” for empty folders; scripts and configs use any relative path.
skills.bundleNeedDirPath: Enter a valid directory path first
skills.bundleDirHint: Creates an empty folder; add files later from the workspace if needed.
skills.bundleFileDirConflict: The same path cannot be both a file and a directory
skills.bundlePathInvalid: Check extra file paths (required, no .., not SKILL.md)
skills.bundlePathTrailingSlash: Path must not end with / — finish the file or folder name
skills.bundleSkillMdReserved: Edit SKILL.md via the main doc tab, not as an extra path
skills.sourcesPanelHint: Manage Git sources and registry; expand sections as needed.
skills.registryHint: Provide a registry URL to search and install public skills.

skillsGit.title: Git Sources
skillsGit.subtitle: Pull skills from Git repositories with manual or scheduled auto-update; synced content lands under .cursor/skills/.
skillsGit.deleteConfirmContent: Files already synced into .cursor/skills/ are kept; only this Git source config is removed.
skillsGit.kindSingleLong: Single bundle: repo root is one skill (contains SKILL.md)
skillsGit.kindMultiLong: Multi bundle: each subdirectory in the repo is one skill
skillsGit.targetSingleExtra: If empty, derived from the repo name (.git stripped)
skillsGit.targetMultiExtra: If empty, scan the repo root for subdirectories containing SKILL.md
skillsGit.authNoneHint: Public repositories do not need authentication; switch later if the repo becomes private.
skillsGit.tokenEnvExtra: Store the token value via the Env editor; only the variable name is persisted in config.
skillsGit.usernameExtra: GitHub / GitLab typically use oauth2; self-hosted varies
skillsGit.sshKeyPathExtra: Key file must be readable by the current user; place it under ~/.ssh/.
skillsGit.sshPassphraseEnvExtra: If the key is encrypted, store the passphrase in .env and reference its name
skillsGit.autoUpdateHint: When enabled, OpenPawlet pulls the latest content in the background at the configured interval
skillsGit.intervalExtra: Recommend at least 15 minutes to avoid hitting remote rate limits

agents.title: Agents
agents.subtitle: Manage multiple AI Agents, each with independent configuration and capabilities
agents.emptyHint: No agents yet — click the button above to create one
agents.hideConfirmDescription: Are you sure you want to hide this Agent?
agents.batchDeleteContent: Are you sure you want to delete {{count}} selected agent(s)? This cannot be undone.
agents.agentNamePlaceholder: e.g. Code Review, Documentation, Test Generation
agents.displayCategoryExtra: Used for list filtering and tag colors; custom categories can be created via + Add Category
agents.displayCategoryExtraEdit: Used for list filtering and tag colors
agents.modelPlaceholder: Select model (defaults to global if empty)
agents.systemPromptPlaceholder: Define the Agent's behavior and personality…
agents.topicsExtra: The agent subscribes to these topic prefixes to receive events from other agents or system components
agents.topicsPlaceholder: e.g. order, inventory.updated, agent.direct (press Enter to add)
agents.topicNamingRule: Naming rules: lowercase letters, digits, ., -, _ only; up to 20 topics and 64 chars each
agents.topicInvalidFormat: Invalid topic format: only lowercase letters, digits, ., -, _ are allowed
agents.topicTooLong: Topic length must be 64 characters or fewer
agents.topicLimitExceeded: You can add at most {{max}} topics
agents.topicValidationFailed: Please fix topic settings before saving
agents.modalAddCategoryHint: New categories appear in the filter tabs. Assign an Agent to a category via the Display Category field when creating or editing.
agents.importUploadText: Click or drag a file to upload

teams.title: Teams
teams.subtitle: Group agents into rooms; each member uses a separate session file. OpenPawlet runs the gateway with the queue broker by default; team/room selection is saved for the next gateway restart.
teams.name: Name
teams.noMembers: No members — add agents from the pool
teams.teamSkillsHelp: Allowlist: only these skills are loaded for members in this team. Intersects with each agent’s skill list. A running OpenPawlet gateway picks this up on the next message (no restart).
teams.deleteRoomDesc: After deletion, this room transcript is no longer available in team view. Room: {{roomId}}
teams.noRooms: Create a room to start a merged session view
teams.transcriptEmpty: No messages in this room yet. Chat with a member below, or restart the gateway after creating teams/rooms so in-process member loops pick up the latest binding.
teams.chatWithMemberHint: Open Chat with this member (restart gateway after team/room changes if loops do not update).
teams.sessionKeysHint: Each member persists under its own console session key in the workspace.
teams.confirmRemoveMember: Remove this member from the team?

queues.title: Queue Manager
queues.subtitle: Live broker metrics, socket topology, and recent message samples.
queues.confirmClear: Clear the dedupe table? Duplicates may slip through briefly.

runtime.title: Runtime Manager
runtime.subtitle: Unified lifecycle for the main agent and subagents. Team agents are plain subagents tagged with a team_id.
runtime.manageInAgentsPage: Disable from the Agent Teams page
runtime.disableHint: Disable the agent on the Agent Teams page; the runtime stops it within ~2s
runtime.unavailable: Runtime manager unavailable (embedded runtime not started or degraded mode)
runtime.confirmStopMain: Stop the main agent loop? Inbound messages will not be processed until restarted.
runtime.confirmStopSub: Stop this subagent? The task will be cancelled immediately.
runtime.fieldTaskPlaceholder: Describe what the subagent should accomplish
runtime.fieldParentExtra: Optional, defaults to the current main agent
runtime.fieldTeamExtra: Optional metadata; no team entity is created
runtime.fieldProfileExtra: Optional. When set, the sub-agent runs with this persona's model / tools / SOUL.
runtime.startProfileTaskPlaceholder: Describe the task for this agent
runtime.viewDetail: View this sub-agent's transcript and status
runtime.detailMainDisabled: Open the Chat page to inspect the main agent
runtime.transcriptUnavailable: No dedicated transcript persisted for this agent (only main or ad-hoc sub-agents).
